html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夢入官署 - Dreamed of entering an official office; a common motif in classical Chinese literature.
  • 祠祀數有靈驗云 - The temple has been repeatedly shown to be effective; indicating the temple's reputation for miracles.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 偽吳 - Refers to the short-lived state of Wuyue, a regional power during the Five Dynasties period.
  • 城隍神 - Refers to the city god, a deity worshipped in Chinese folk religion.
  • 宋無忌 - A historical figure, often associated with Taoist mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 稽神錄 - The title of the text, meaning 'Record of Spirits' or 'Inquiry into Spirits.'
  • 徐鉉 - The author of the text.
  • 揚都 - The city of Yangzhou.
  • 安福 - The place where Zhu Zheng was appointed as magistrate.
  • 宋無忌廟 - The Temple of Song Wujì.
  • 廣記 - A reference to the 'Encyclopedia of the Mysterious,' a collection of supernatural stories.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嘗 - In this context, it means 'once' or 'at one time,' referring to a past event.
  • 逾年 - Means 'after a year' or 'the following year,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 泄其事 - Means 'to reveal the story' or 'to disclose the matter.'
  • 失劍 - Means 'to lose the sword,' indicating the disappearance of the sword after the story was revealed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鄭就 - The name of the poor butcher who finds the sword.
  • 廉頗 - A famous general from the Warring States period, known for his military prowess and loyalty.
  • 大王 - A respectful term for a powerful ruler or leader, often used in historical contexts.
  • 天命 - Refers to the Mandate of Heaven, a concept in Chinese philosophy that justifies the rule of a just and virtuous ruler.
  • 天子 - A term for the emperor, indicating the highest authority in the state.
  • 生祠 - A temple or shrine built in honor of a living person, often a hero or a revered figure.
  • 立壇醮上帝 - Refers to the act of setting up an altar and performing a ritual to worship the supreme deity.
  • 天符 - A divine sign or message from heaven, often interpreted as a sign of divine approval or guidance.
  • 碧紙朱書 - Describes the appearance of the divine message, with blue paper and red writing.
  • 王守貞 - A name, possibly a nickname or title, indicating a person known for their skills and craftsmanship.
  • 俗謂之 - Means 'commonly called' or 'known as,' indicating a popular name or title.

Proper Nouns

  • 鄭就 - The name of the poor butcher.
  • 廉頗 - The name of the famous general from the Warring States period.
  • 董昌 - The name of the person who becomes the emperor.
  • 山陰縣 - A county in the region of Shanxi, where the story takes place.
  • 王守貞 - The name of the skilled craftsman.
  • 朱思遠 - The name of the Taoist priest who performs the ritual.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妖伪之际 - A time of chaos or false pretenses, often associated with periods of political or social instability.
  • 尤兴百艺幻惑之术 - Especially flourished various arts of illusion and deception, referring to the proliferation of fraudulent practices during troubled times.
  • 万世之业利在于此 - The benefit for the millennia lies in this, emphasizing the long-term significance of the event.
  • 僭衮冕仪卫 - Wearing the ceremonial robes and insignia of a high official or ruler, an act of usurpation.
  • 登子城门楼，赦境内 - Ascended the city gate tower and proclaimed amnesty throughout the domain, indicating a formal declaration of authority.
  • 改伪号罗平国，年号天册 - Changed the false title to the state of Luo Ping and the era name to Tian Ce, indicating a change in the state's name and the ruler's reign title.
  • 自称圣人 - Proclaimed himself a sage, indicating a claim to divine or semi-divine status.
  • 万岁 - A common exclamation of respect or praise, often used for emperors or rulers.
  • 俯而曰言云云 - Bowed and said various things, indicating a formal speech or declaration.
  • 压得朕头疼无奈何也 - It's pressing on my head and I can't help it, a humorous way of saying that the speech was too long or too much for the speaker to handle.
  • 盖缘工人所制平天冠稍重 - Because the hat made by the craftsmen was a bit heavy, providing a humorous explanation for the speaker's complaint.
  • 时人闻者皆大笑之 - Those who heard it all laughed at him, indicating that the event was met with ridicule.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妖伪之际 - Refers to a period of political or social instability, often associated with false pretenses or usurpations.
  • 百艺幻惑之术 - Refers to various fraudulent or deceptive practices that were common during times of chaos.
  • 谶 - Refers to a prophecy or omen, often associated with divination or fortune-telling.
  • 卯 - The hour of the rabbit in the Chinese zodiac, associated with the element of wood and the direction east.
  • 子城门楼 - Refers to the city gate tower, a significant structure in ancient Chinese cities.
  • 赦境内 - Refers to the act of proclaiming amnesty throughout the domain, a common practice in ancient Chinese governance.
  • 伪号 - Refers to a false title or name, often used by usurpers or pretenders.
  • 年号 - Refers to the era name, a title used by Chinese emperors to mark the beginning of a new reign.
  • 圣人 - Refers to a sage or wise person, often associated with Confucian ideals.
  • 万岁 - A common exclamation of respect or praise, often used for emperors or rulers.
  • 平天冠 - Refers to a ceremonial hat worn by high officials or rulers, often associated with imperial authority.
  • 时人 - Refers to the people of the time, indicating the contemporary audience's reaction to the event.

Proper Nouns

  • 昌 - The name of the person who proclaimed himself a sage and claimed to be the subject of a prophecy.
  • 罗平国 - The false title of the state that was proclaimed by the speaker.
  • 天册 - The era name that was proclaimed by the speaker.
  • 圣人 - The title that the speaker claimed for himself.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 岸崩出 - Literally means 'the bank collapsed and exposed,' indicating that an ancient tomb was revealed due to a landslide.
  • 藤蔓缠其棺 - Describes a vine wrapping around the coffin, a metaphorical or literal description of the state of the tomb.
  • 欲陷不陷被藤缚，欲落不落被沙阁 - Part of the inscription, using a series of paradoxes to describe the tomb's condition.
  • 至建州刺史 - Indicates that Xiong Bo later became the governor of Jianzhou.
  • 雨雹方甚 - Describes a severe hailstorm.
  • 震地 - Means 'shaking the ground,' describing the intensity of the thunder.
  • 下一大雹于街中 - Describes a large hailstone falling in the street.
  • 其高广与寺楼等 - Means 'its height and width were equal to the temple tower,' emphasizing the size of the hailstone.
  • 入地可丈余 - Describes the depth of the hole left by the hailstone.
  • 炎风赫日 - Refers to hot winds and blazing sun, indicating a sudden change in weather.
  • 经月雹乃消尽 - Means 'after a month, the hail finally melted away.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 熊博 - A historical figure from Jian'an, whose actions and later career are described.
  • 建安 - A county in Fujian Province, China.
  • 建州 - An ancient administrative region in Fujian Province.
  • 彭城 - An ancient name for present-day Xuzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 天祐初 - The early years of the Tianyou era, a period during the late Tang dynasty.
  • 平光寺 - A Buddhist temple, possibly located in Jian'an.
  • 佛寺 - Refers to Buddhist temples, which were important cultural and religious centers in ancient China.
  • 雨雹 - Hailstorms, which are significant natural phenomena often recorded in historical texts.
  • 炎风赫日 - Hot winds and blazing sun, indicating a sudden change in weather conditions.
  • 经月雹乃消尽 - The passage of time and the eventual melting of the hailstone, reflecting the transient nature of natural events.

Proper Nouns

  • 熊博 - The name of the subject.
  • 建安 - A county in Fujian Province.
  • 建州 - An ancient administrative region in Fujian Province.
  • 彭城 - An ancient name for present-day Xuzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 平光寺 - A Buddhist temple.
  • 佛寺 - Buddhist temples.
  • 天祐初 - The early years of the Tianyou era.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居决守寺之前 - Lived in front of the Deciding and Guarding Temple; the exact nature of this temple is unclear.
  • 少遇乱失其父母 - Lost her parents in the chaos when she was young.
  • 若尔 - Colloquial expression meaning 'if so' or 'if that is the case.'
  • 权寄门下信宿 - Temporarily stay at your place for two nights.
  • 暴风雨从南方来 - A violent storm came from the south, implying a sudden and unexpected event.
  • 大水平地数尺 - Flood waters rose several feet above the ground, indicating a severe flood.
  • 后数日 - Several days later.
  • 欧阳之人至后土庙神座前得一书 - A person from the Ouyang family found a book at the altar of the Earth God temple.
  • 老父讼女文也 - The text was the father's complaint against his daughter, but the exact nature of the complaint is unclear.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 广陵 - Refers to Guangling, an ancient city in present-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 孔目吏 - Refers to a low-ranking official or clerk in the government.
  • 欧阳氏 - Refers to the Ouyang family, a prominent family in Chinese history.
  • 决守寺 - The name of a temple, but its exact historical significance is unclear.
  • 后土庙 - Refers to a temple dedicated to the Earth God, a deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 后土庙神座前 - Refers to the altar of the Earth God temple, a place of worship and ritual.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '少遇乱' is ambiguous. It could mean 'lost her parents in the chaos when she was young' or 'lost her parents due to a young age.' The context suggests the former.
  • The phrase '权寄门下信宿' is elliptical and requires context to understand fully. It means 'temporarily stay at your place for two nights.'
  • The phrase '去吾将讼尔矣' is a threat, meaning 'I will sue you if you go.'
  • The sentence '大水平地数尺，邻里皆漂荡不自持' describes a severe flood, but the exact extent and impact are not clear from the text alone.
  • The sentence '后数日，欧阳之人至后土庙神座前得一书' is a bit ambiguous. It could mean 'several days later, a person from the Ouyang family found a book at the altar of the Earth God temple' or 'several days later, the Ouyang family found a book at the altar of the Earth God temple.'

Proper Nouns

  • 欧阳某 - The name of the clerk, but the exact identity is unknown.
  • 欧阳氏 - The Ouyang family, a prominent family in Chinese history.
  • 后土庙 - The Earth God temple, a place of worship and ritual.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庐山卖油者 - Literally 'the oil seller of Mount Lu,' referring to a person who sells oil in the vicinity of Mount Lu.
  • 养其母甚孝谨 - Means 'he was very filial and respectful to his mother.'
  • 为暴雷震死 - Means 'was struck dead by a violent thunderbolt.'
  • 日号泣于九天使者之祠 - Means 'daily wept and cried at the temple of the Nine Heavenly Messengers.'
  • 愿知其故 - Means 'wished to know the reason.'
  • 汝子恒以鱼膏杂油中，以图厚利 - Means 'your son always mixed fish fat into the oil to gain greater profit.'
  • 腥气薰蒸，灵仙不降 - Means 'the stench of fish fat was so strong that it prevented the spirits from descending.'
  • 母知其事，遂止 - Means 'the mother learned of this and stopped the practice.'
  • 江南军使苏建雄 - Refers to Su Jianxiong, a military governor in southern China.
  • 有别墅在毗陵 - Means 'had a villa in Piling (modern-day Changzhou).'
  • 乙卯岁（案：乙卯当周显德二年） - Refers to the year 955 CE, during the Zhou Dynasty.
  • 时盛暑赫日 - Means 'it was a hot summer day.'
  • 持伞自覆 - Means 'carried an umbrella to shield himself.'
  • 卷其伞盖而去 - Means 'carried away his umbrella.'
  • 惟持伞柄，行数十步 - Means 'he only held the umbrella handle and walked several steps.'
  • 忽有飘席至其所 - Means 'suddenly a floating mat arrived there.'
  • 因取覆之 - Means 'he took it and used it to cover himself.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庐山 - A famous mountain in Jiangxi Province, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 九天使者之祠 - Refers to a temple dedicated to the Nine Heavenly Messengers, a group of deities in Chinese mythology.
  • 乙卯岁 - A specific year in the Chinese lunar calendar, corresponding to 955 CE.
  • 毗陵 - An ancient name for Changzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 傔人 - Refers to a servant or attendant.

Proper Nouns

  • 庐山 - Mount Lu.
  • 九天使者之祠 - Temple of the Nine Heavenly Messengers.
  • 乙卯岁 - The year 955 CE.
  • 毗陵 - Piling (modern-day Changzhou).
  • 苏建雄 - Su Jianxiong.
  • 李诚 - Li Cheng.
  • 句容县 - Jurong County.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄而 - Temporal adverb meaning 'soon after' or 'in a short while.'
  • 卷去 - Literally 'roll away,' used here to describe a sudden and dramatic event.
  • 誠又西行數里 - Means 'Cheng again walked several miles westward.' The repetition of '又' (again) suggests a continuation of a previous action.
  • 求買 - Literally 'seek to buy,' indicating a request or desire to purchase something.
  • 異之 - Means 'found it strange' or 'was puzzled by it.'
  • 但求乞甚切 - Means 'only begged very earnestly,' emphasizing the urgency of the request.
  • 隨行數百步 - Means 'followed for several hundred steps,' indicating a distance traveled together.
  • 乃去 - Means 'then left,' marking the end of an interaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷震地道傍數家之中 - Refers to a thunderstorm that struck a section of an underground passage, likely an ancient tunnel or cave system.
  • 其居荡然无复遗者 - Indicates that the dwelling place was completely destroyed, leaving nothing behind.
  • 老幼十余皆聚桑林中，一无所伤 - Highlights the miraculous survival of the family members, who gathered in a mulberry grove unharmed.
  • 舍前有足迹，长三尺 - Mentions footprints left behind, which are unusually large and significant in the context of the story.
  • 诚又西行数里 - The character '诚' (cheng) is likely a proper name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character in the narrative.
  • 遇一人求买所覆席 - Describes an encounter where someone asks to buy the cover of a mat, suggesting a practical or mundane transaction.

Proper Nouns

  • 诚 - Likely a proper name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character in the narrative.
  • 《广记》 - Refers to the 'Guangji' (Widely Recorded), a collection of stories and anecdotes from the Tang Dynasty.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庚寅岁 - Specific year in the Chinese sexagenary cycle; corresponds to 930 CE in the Gregorian calendar, the first year of the Longxing era during the Later Tang dynasty.
  • 假寐 - To take a short nap or doze off briefly.
  • 喧竞 - To quarrel or argue loudly.
  • 匍匐 - To crawl or creep, often used to describe a person struggling to move.
  • 数四 - Several times or repeatedly.
  • 震死其牛 - The thunderbolt killed the cow; describes a sudden and violent death.
  • 汗衫乃自牛口中呕出 - The sweatshirt was then vomited out of the cow's mouth; describes the act of spitting out or regurgitating something.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庚寅岁 - The year 930 CE, during the Later Tang dynasty, specifically the first year of the Longxing era.
  • 茅山 - Mount Mao, a mountain in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 大和中 - The Dahe period (827-835) of the Tang dynasty, a time of relative peace and prosperity.
  • 长兴元年 - The first year of the Changxing era (930 CE) of the Later Tang dynasty.
  • 吴大和二年 - The second year of the Dahu period (929 CE) of the Wu state, a regional power during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 雷雨暴至 - A sudden and violent storm, often associated with divine intervention in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 庚寅岁 - The year 930 CE, during the Changxing era of the Later Tang dynasty.
  • 大和中 - The Dahe period (827-835) of the Tang dynasty.
  • 长兴元年 - The first year of the Changxing era (930 CE) of the Later Tang dynasty.
  • 吴大和二年 - The second year of the Dahu period (929 CE) of the Wu state.
  • 茅山 - Mount Mao, a mountain in Jiangsu Province.
  • 曾慥《类说》 - A collection of anecdotes and stories compiled by Zeng Zao.
  • 《广记》 - A reference to the 'Guangji' (Widely Recorded), a collection of stories and legends.
  • 邻儿 - The neighboring child.
  • 汗衫 - A sweatshirt or shirt that has been worn and is damp with sweat.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庚申岁 - Traditional Chinese calendar reference meaning 'the year of the Gengshen cycle.' In this context, it is identified as the first year of the Song Dynasty (建隆元年, 960 AD).
  • 番禺村 - Refers to a village in Fanyu, a historical region in modern-day Guangdong Province.
  • 老姥 - Refers to an elderly woman, often used to describe a grandmother or an older female relative.
  • 饷田 - A traditional agricultural practice where food is carried to the fields for the workers.
  • 云雨晦冥 - A poetic expression meaning 'dark clouds and rain,' often used to describe a stormy or ominous weather condition.
  • 霁 - Means 'clearing' or 'after the rain has stopped.'
  • 鹿脯、乾鱼、果实、酒醢 - Refers to various food items: venison jerky, dried fish, fruits, and alcoholic condiments.
  • 甚丰腆 - Means 'very abundant and rich.'
  • 盛服而至 - Means 'arriving in fine attire.'
  • 雷师所娶 - Refers to being taken by a thunder god as a wife.
  • 石室 - Refers to a cave or a stone chamber.
  • 亲族甚众 - Means 'a large number of relatives.'
  • 婚姻之礼，一同人间 - Means 'marriage rituals, the same as in human society.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庚申岁（案：庚申当宋建隆元年） - The year 960 AD, the first year of the Song Dynasty.
  • 番禺村 - A historical village in Fanyu, which is now part of Guangzhou, Guangdong Province.
  • 雷师 - Refers to a thunder god, a deity associated with thunder and lightning in Chinese mythology.
  • 建隆 - The era name of Emperor Taizu of Song, the first emperor of the Song Dynasty.
  • 庭中陈列筵席 - Describes a scene where a banquet is set up in the courtyard, a common practice in traditional Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '忽云雨晦冥，及霁，乃失其女' describes a sudden change in weather followed by the disappearance of the girl. The structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '留数宿，一夕复风雨晦冥，遂不可见矣' describes the girl staying for several nights and then disappearing again during another storm. The sequence of events and the use of '遂' (thus) need to be carefully rendered to maintain the narrative flow.

Proper Nouns

  • 番禺村 - A historical village in Fanyu, Guangdong Province.
  • 雷师 - A thunder god in Chinese mythology.
  • 石室 - A cave or stone chamber.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 江西村妪 - Literal descriptive phrase meaning 'an old woman from a village in Jiangxi.'
  • 雷震 - Means 'thunderclap' or 'thunderbolt,' often used to describe sudden, powerful events.
  • 电火 - Refers to lightning, which is described as burning the woman's arm.
  • 空中有呼曰 - Means 'there was a voice calling from the air,' indicating a supernatural event.
  • 误矣 - A colloquial expression meaning 'mistake' or 'error.'
  • 以此傅之 - Means 'use this to apply it,' referring to the application of the medicine.
  • 即瘥 - Means 'immediately recover' or 'immediately heal.'
  • 将取藏之 - Means 'to take and store it,' indicating the intention to keep the medicine.
  • 数人共举其瓶不能动 - Means 'several people tried to lift the bottle but could not move it,' emphasizing the unusual nature of the bottle.
  • 震死 - Means 'killed by thunder,' indicating the cause of death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江西 - Refers to Jiangxi Province, a region in southeastern China.
  • 村妪 - Refers to an old woman from a rural area, highlighting the common folk setting.
  • 雷震 - In Chinese culture, thunder is often associated with divine intervention or supernatural events.
  • 电火 - Lightning is often seen as a manifestation of divine power or a sign of something significant.
  • 空中有呼曰 - The voice from the air is a common motif in Chinese folklore, often associated with gods or spirits.
  • 误矣 - This phrase is used to indicate a mistake, which is a common theme in stories of divine intervention.
  • 以此傅之 - The use of a specific medicine or remedy is a common element in traditional Chinese medicine stories.
  • 即瘥 - Immediate recovery is a common theme in stories of miraculous healing.
  • 将取藏之 - The desire to keep a miraculous object is a common human response in such stories.
  • 数人共举其瓶不能动 - The inability to move the bottle is a supernatural element that emphasizes its unusual nature.
  • 震死 - Death by thunder is a dramatic and significant event in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 江西 - Jiangxi Province.
  • 村妪 - An old woman from a village.
  • 雷震 - Thunderclap.
  • 电火 - Lightning.
  • 误矣 - Mistake.
  • 以此傅之 - Apply this.
  • 即瘥 - Recover immediately.
  • 将取藏之 - Take and store.
  • 数人共举其瓶不能动 - Several people tried to lift the bottle but could not move it.
  • 震死 - Killed by thunder.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 怒形于色 - Means 'anger showing on the face,' describing the visible emotion.
  • 其光如电 - Means 'his light like lightning,' describing a bright and intense gaze.
  • 俄而 - Means 'soon after' or 'shortly thereafter,' indicating a brief passage of time.
  • 向者 - Means 'just now' or 'recently,' indicating a recent event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 甘露寺 - A specific temple in Chinese history, often associated with Taoist practices and legends.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest or practitioner, often depicted in classical literature as wise and sometimes supernatural.
  • 褐衣 - Coarse clothing, often associated with simplicity and humility.
  • 帔 - A type of cloak or shawl, often worn by women or monks.
  • 霹雳 - Thunderbolt, often associated with lightning and divine power.
  • 龙 - Dragon, a symbol of power and divine intervention in Chinese mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 甘露寺 - A specific temple in Chinese history.
  • 范可保 - The name of the Taoist priest.
  • 浙西 - A region in Zhejiang Province, China.

[12]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 置酒 - Means 'to set up a feast' or 'to prepare wine and food.'
  • 手掩目 - Literally 'to cover one's eyes with one's hand,' indicating a protective gesture.
  • 蔌蔌有声 - Describes a rustling or rustling sound.
  • 良久 - Means 'for a long time' or 'after a while.'
  • 微反 - Means 'slightly tilted' or 'slightly overturned.'
  • 银酒杯与杯之舟皆狭长 - Describes the silver wine cups and their bases as narrow and long.
  • 迅雷 - Means 'thunderbolt' or 'loud thunder.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南康县令 - Refers to the magistrate of Nankang County.
  • 胡侃 - The name of the magistrate.
  • 莲华馆 - The name of the pavilion where the event took place.
  • 建隆二年 - The second year of the Jianlong era, which corresponds to 961 CE.
  • 盘中器物 - Refers to items on the table, such as plates and bowls.
  • 影壁 - Refers to a wall or screen in front of a building, often used for decorative purposes.
  • 墙外 - Refers to outside the wall.

Proper Nouns

  • 南康县 - Nankang County.
  • 胡侃 - The name of the magistrate.
  • 莲华馆 - Lotus Pavilion.
  • 建隆二年 - The second year of the Jianlong era (961 CE).

[13]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • ○犬吠石 - Literal title meaning 'Barking Stone,' referring to a stone that causes dogs to bark.
  • ○瓮形石 - Literal title meaning 'Jar-Shaped Stone,' referring to a stone shaped like a jar.
  • 自山墜於溪側 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'fell from the mountain to the side of the stream.'
  • 瑩徹可愛 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'clear and lovely.'
  • 群犬見而競吠之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the dogs saw it and barked at it together.'
  • 數日，村人不堪其喧 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'after several days, the villagers could not bear the noise.'
  • 乃相與推致水中 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'they then pushed it into the water together.'
  • 俯水而吠 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'barked while bending over the water.'
  • 愈急取而碎之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'became more urgent and took it to break it.'
  • 犬乃不吠 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the dogs then stopped barking.'

  • 数年，浙西乱，王出奔，至蜀下，与乡人夜会 - Sequence of events: passage of time, political upheaval, and gathering of people.
  • 语及青蚨（毛本“青蚨”二字空阙）西送（《广记》无“送”字）还钱事 - Sequence of conversation and allusion: discussion about a mythical insect and sending something westward.
  • 坐中或云“人欲求富，莫如得石中金蚕蓄之，则宝货自至（《广记》作“致”）矣” - Sequence of suggestion and allusion: suggestion made by someone in the gathering.
  • 问其形状，则石中蛴螬也 - Sequence of question and answer: question about the shape of the silkworm and the answer.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure 酒家召井工陶老, 有（毛本作“其”）工人父子应募者 is clear but includes a variant reading in the note.
  • The crowd's reaction 观者如堵, 无敢复入 emphasizes the fear and mystery.

  • The community's reaction to the event 临井而哭, 无敢入者.
  • The butcher's attempt to investigate and the supernatural barrier 有屠儿请入视之, 但见大釜，汤涌（《广记》作“沸”）火炽, 有人拒其足曰：“事不干汝。”不得入而出.

  • 乃先杀之 - Means 'he then killed him first'; this phrase emphasizes the sequence of events.
  • 而（《广记》有“后”字）宣赦 - Means 'and (afterward) the amnesty was announced'; the note from *Guangji* suggests adding '后' (afterward) for clarity.

  • 乃见盗者 - Means 'he then saw the thief'; this indicates the thief appeared unexpectedly.

  • 我今得请于所司矣 - Means 'I have now appealed to the authorities'; '所司' means the authorities or officials.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 常使一小奴出（《广记》有“入”字）城市 - Often sends a small servant to (or from) the city. The inclusion of '入' in the *Guangji* version adds complexity, suggesting the servant might also enter the city.
  • 经过（《广记》无“过”字）舍南大冢 - Passes by (or through) the large tomb south of his house. The omission of '过' in the *Guangji* version changes the nuance slightly.
  • 冢旁恒有一黄衣人（《广记》作“儿”）与之较力为戏 - There is always a person dressed in yellow (or a child) by the tomb who plays wrestling games with him. The variation in *Guangji* suggests the figure might be younger.
  • 其主因归迟将责之（《广记》无“归”字及“将责”二字） - The master returns late and is about to scold him (or him). The *Guangji* version omits '归' and '将责,' altering the sentence structure.
  • 奴以实告，往（《广记》无“往”字）觇之，信然 - The servant tells the truth, goes (or checks) to see, and indeed finds it to be true. The *Guangji* version lacks '往,' which affects the flow.
  • 一日挟挝而往 - One day, he takes a stick and goes. '挝' refers to a type of club or stick.
  • 伏于草间 - Hides in the grass.
  • 小奴至，黄衣儿复出，即起击之，应手而仆（《广记》作“踣”） - When the small servant arrives, the yellow-clad child comes out again, and he immediately hits him, causing him to fall (or collapse). '踣' in *Guangji* emphasizes the fall more dramatically.
  • 乃金儿也 - It turns out to be a golden child.
  • 因持以归，家遂殷（《广记》“遂殷”作“自是”）富 - He then takes it home, and his family becomes prosperous (or from then on). The *Guangji* version uses '自是,' which means 'from then on,' adding a temporal aspect.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 暑夜坐鎮門外納涼 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'sitting outside the gate during a hot night to cool off.' It conveys a sense of leisure and observation.
  • 忽見道南桑林中，有白衣婦人獨舞 - Vivid description of a sudden, unexpected sight: 'suddenly seeing a white-clad woman dancing alone in the mulberry grove to the south of the road.' Emphasizes the suddenness and surprise of the encounter.
  • 就視即滅 - Means 'when approached, it vanished.' Suggests a supernatural or fleeting nature of the apparition.
  • 扶（《廣記》作“挾”）杖先往 - '扶' means 'to support' or 'to lean on,' but the *Guangji* suggests '挾' (to carry or hold). The choice of word affects the tone: '扶' implies a more gentle or cautious approach, while '挾' suggests a more determined or prepared action.
  • 伏於草間 - Means 'to lie hidden in the grass,' indicating a stealthy and patient waiting.
  • 婦人復出而（《廣記》作“方”）舞 - '復出' means 'to reappear,' and the *Guangji* suggests '方' (just then or immediately). The choice of word affects the timing: '復出' emphasizes the reappearance after a pause, while '方' suggests immediate continuation.
  • 即擊之墜（《廣記》作“墮”）地 - '墜' means 'to fall,' and the *Guangji* suggests '墮' (to drop or fall). Both words convey the same basic meaning, but '墜' has a slightly more dramatic connotation.
  • 乃白金一餅（《廣記》作“<缶並>”） - '白金' means 'white metal,' which is often used to refer to silver. The *Guangji* uses '<缶並>' (a rare character), which could be a typo or a variant. The choice of word affects the clarity: '白金' is more commonly understood, while '<缶並>' is less familiar.
  • 復掘地，獲銀數（《廣記》無“數”字）千兩 - '復掘地' means 'to dig again,' and the *Guangji* omits '數' (number). The omission affects the specificity: '獲銀數千兩' is more precise, while '獲銀千兩' is less specific but still clear.
  • 遂致富裕云（《廣記》卷四百一，“致”作“為”，“裕”作“人”） - '遂致富裕' means 'thus became wealthy and prosperous.' The *Guangji* uses '為' (became) and '人' (a person), which changes the meaning slightly: '遂為人' means 'thus became a person' or 'thus became wealthy.' The choice of words affects the interpretation: '遂致富裕' emphasizes wealth and prosperity, while '遂為人' is more ambiguous.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建州（《广记》作“安”）村人 - Refers to villagers from Jianzhou (or Anzhou, as noted in the *Guangji*); the inconsistency between the two names requires clarification in translation.
  • 乘小舟，往来建溪中，卖薪为业 - Describes the villagers' occupation of traveling on the Jian River to sell firewood.
  • 忽见山上有数百钱流下 - Can be interpreted as either a large number of coins flowing down or a smaller number of coins moving in a stream-like manner.
  • 稍上寻之，累获数十 - Gradually went up and collected dozens in succession.
  • 未（《广记》作“可”）及山半 - The *Guangji* version uses '可' instead of '未,' changing the meaning slightly; '未及' means 'not yet reached,' while '可及' means 'could reach.'
  • 有大树下一（《广记》“一”字作“有大”二字）瓮 - The *Guangji* version adds '有大' before '瓮,' changing the description of the jar; '瓮' refers to a large earthenware jar.
  • 钱满其中，而瓮小欹 - '钱满其中' means 'the jar was full of coins,' and '瓮小欹' means 'the jar was slightly tilted.'
  • 于是推而正之，以石支之 - '推而正之' means 'pushed it upright,' and '以石支之' means 'used a stone to prop it up.'
  • 纳衣襟得（《广记》“纳”作“以”，“得”作“贮”）五百而归 - The *Guangji* version changes '纳' to '以' and '得' to '贮,' altering the description of how the coins were carried; '纳衣襟得' means 'stuffed them into his sleeves and collected five hundred.'
  • 尽率其（《广记》无“其”字）家人复往（《广记》有将字）尽取 - The *Guangji* version omits '其' and adds '将' before '复往,' changing the phrasing; '尽率其家人复往' means 'led his entire family back to take all of them.'
  • 既至，得旧路，见大树，而亡其瓮 - '既至' means 'after arriving,' '得旧路' means 'found the old path,' '见大树' means 'saw the large tree,' and '亡其瓮' means 'lost the jar.'
  • 村人徘徊数日不能去 - '徘徊数日' means 'wandered for several days,' and '不能去' means 'could not leave.'
  • 夜梦人告之曰 - '夜梦人' means 'in a dream, a person,' and '告之曰' means 'told him, saying.'
  • 此钱有主，向为瓮欹，以五百雇而（《广记》而作尔）正之，不可再得也（《广记》，“再得”作“妄想”，“不可”上有“余”字） - The *Guangji* version changes '而' to '尔' and '再得' to '妄想,' and adds '余' before '不可'; '此钱有主' means 'these coins have an owner,' '向为瓮欹' means 'before, the jar was tilted,' '以五百雇而正之' means 'with five hundred coins, he had it fixed,' '不可再得也' means 'it cannot be obtained again,' and '妄想' means 'a delusion.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 建安 - Jian'an was a district in Fujian Province during the Eastern Han period, known for its rich cultural heritage.
  • 建溪 - The Jian River, a tributary of the Min River, flows through Jian'an.
  • 《广记》 - The *Guangji* (Tales of the Strange) is a collection of supernatural tales compiled by Li Fang during the Song Dynasty.
  • 瓮 - A large earthenware jar commonly used for storing grain or other goods in ancient China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The inconsistency between '建州' and '安' in '建州（《广记》作“安”）村人' requires careful handling to maintain clarity in translation.
  • The phrase '忽见山上有数百钱流下' can be interpreted as either a large number of coins flowing down or a smaller number of coins moving in a stream-like manner.
  • The difference between '未' and '可' in '未（《广记》作“可”）及山半' affects the interpretation of whether the villagers reached the halfway point or could have reached it.
  • The addition of '有大' in '有大树下一（《广记》“一”字作“有大”二字）瓮' changes the description of the jar.
  • The changes in the *Guangji* version of '纳衣襟得（《广记》“纳”作“以”，“得”作“贮”）五百而归' affect the description of how the coins were carried.
  • The omission of '其' and the addition of '将' in '尽率其（《广记》无“其”字）家人复往（《广记》有将字）尽取' change the phrasing.
  • The sequence of events in '既至，得旧路，见大树，而亡其瓮' must be clearly articulated to avoid confusion.
  • The dream narrative in '夜梦人告之曰' requires careful handling to convey the supernatural element.

  • 《广记》 - The *Guangji* (Tales of the Strange).
  • 瓮 - A large earthenware jar.

  • The phrase '忽闻扣门者，人马之声甚众' can be interpreted as a sudden and loud noise of many people and horses knocking on the door, creating a sense of urgency and surprise.

  • The sentence '余身为地府南曹判官，奉王命召君为北曹判官尔' is clear but contains a formal and somewhat mysterious tone, typical of classical Chinese narratives involving supernatural elements.
  • The transition from surprise to calmness is abrupt but effective in conveying the character's emotional state.
  • The rhetorical question emphasizes the importance and rarity of the position, while the command to accept it is direct and authoritative.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 虐其所使婢 - This phrase is elliptical and could be expanded to 'he abused the maid he used.'
  • 即拔取濯水中，连根食之，甚美 - This sequence of actions is compact and can be read as 'immediately pulled it out and washed it in the water, ate the root, and it was very delicious.'
  • 自是恒食，久之遂不饥，而更轻健 - This clause is elliptical and can be expanded to 'from then on, always ate, and after a long time, she no longer felt hungry and became lighter and stronger.'
  • 夜息大树下，闻草中兽走，以为虎而惧 - This sequence of events is compact and can be read as 'slept under a big tree at night, heard animals running in the grass, thought it was a tiger and was afraid.'
  • 因念得上树杪乃生也，正尔念之，而身已在树杪矣 - This clause is elliptical and can be expanded to 'thought that if he could get up to the top of the tree, he would live, and just as he thought that, his body was already at the top of the tree.'
  • 及晓又念当下平地，又效然而下 - This clause is elliptical and can be expanded to 'by morning, he thought about descending to the ground, and effortlessly descended.'
  • 数岁，其家人伐薪见之，以告其主，使捕之不得 - This sequence of events is compact and can be read as 'after several years, his family members who were cutting firewood saw her, reported it to the master, and the master tried to capture her but failed.'
  • 一日遇其在绝壁下 - This clause is elliptical and can be expanded to 'one day, he encountered her at the bottom of a steep cliff.'

  • 众皆惧 - Means 'everyone was afraid.' The term '惧' (afraid) is a straightforward expression of fear.

  • 乃所谓笪却日也 - Means 'it was what they call the '笪却日' (a day when the sun is blocked).' The term '笪却日' (笪却日) is a specific term used to describe a rare celestial phenomenon.

  • 又云，常于无定河见人脑骨一条，大如柱，长可七尺云 - Translates to 'he also said that he often saw a human skull bone on the Wuding River, as large as a pillar and about seven feet long.' The term '无定河' (Wuding River) is a specific river in the region.
